commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b4a7f69c677b34e32dc3dce7e6e30223ef0a80d8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `requiresReasoningContentOnAssistantMessages` to the model compat config schema/type and adds schema tests for accepting that key while still rejecting unknown compat keys.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +45. Total +47 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: the PR would make the config key parse, but `buildOpenAICompletionsParams()` still receives a compat object whose `requiresReasoningContentOnAssistantMessages` value comes only from detection.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can preserve the schema change but must wire the same compat override into the agent transport before this PR can pass review.

target.title: fix(config): allow compat.requiresReasoningContentOnAssistantMessages in model config
target.body:
Fixes #89660.

## Summary

`compat.requiresReasoningContentOnAssistantMessages` is already honored at
runtime — `getCompat()` / `detectCompat()` in `openai-completions.ts` inject
`reasoning_content: ""` on assistant messages when it is set (required by
DeepSeek's API). But the field was missing from the strict `ModelCompatSchema`
and from the `ModelCompatConfig` `Pick`, so setting it to replicate native
DeepSeek behavior on a custom OpenAI-compatible proxy failed gateway-startup
config validation with `Unrecognized key(s)`.

This exposes the existing runtime flag at the config boundary:
- `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts` — add the key to `ModelCompatSchema`.
- `src/config/types.models.ts` — add it to `SupportedOpenAICompatFields`.

Additive and optional. Existing configs are unaffected, and the strict schema
still rejects unknown compat keys.

Only this one field is exposed (it has a concrete reported use case). Other
compat fields that have drifted out of the schema are left as-is, per the
repo's high bar on config surface.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: configuring `compat.requiresReasoningContentOnAssistantMessages: true` on a custom `openai-completions` provider no longer fails config validation.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw build (`pnpm openclaw config validate`, the same schema gateway startup uses), macOS.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Wrote the issue's exact config to `/tmp/issue-89660.json` (my-proxy provider, deepseek-v4-pro model, `compat: { thinkingFormat: "deepseek", requiresReasoningContentOnAssistantMessages: true }`).
  2. `OPENCLAW_FORCE_BUILD=1 OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/issue-89660.json pnpm openclaw config validate --json`
  3. Reverted the two source edits, forced a rebuild, and re-ran the same command to capture the pre-fix failure.
Evidence after fix:
```
After fix:  {"valid":true,"path":"/tmp/issue-89660.json"}
Before fix: OpenClaw config is invalid: /tmp/issue-89660.json
              × models.providers.my-proxy.models.0.compat: Invalid input   (exit 1)
```
Observed result after fix: config accepted (`valid: true`); gateway startup would no longer reject it.
What was not tested: a live end-to-end request against a real DeepSeek-compatible proxy — the runtime injection of `reasoning_content` is pre-existing behavior already covered by `openai-completions-compat.test.ts`; this PR only unblocks configuring it.

## Tests

- `src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts` — adds 2 tests: the field is accepted, and unknown compat keys are still rejected (strict schema preserved).
- Local: `zod-schema.models.test.ts`, `schema.test.ts`, `openai-completions-compat.test.ts`, `schema.base.generated.test.ts`, `doc-baseline.test.ts` all pass; `pnpm tsgo:core` clean; `pnpm check:base-config-schema` ok.
target.author: draix
